key_departures.impact: Traded to Cowboys for a 2027 4th-round pick. Gary had zero sacks or TFLs in the final nine games of 2025. While his production had cratered, he was still the nominal EDGE starter opposite Micah Parsons. Departure further thins an already desperate pass-rush room, especially with Parsons (torn ACL) likely to miss early-season games.
Signed Jets to a 1-year, $10M deal. Enagbare had 11.5 sacks over four Packers seasons and was a valuable rotational/fill-in starter. His departure compounds the already thin EDGE room.

Signed Raiders to a 3-year, $40.5M deal ($28M guaranteed). Walker was Green Bay's leading tackler in all four of his pro seasons (100+ tackles each year) and a 4-year first-round pick investment. Departure leaves a hole at off-ball LB opposite Edgerrin Cooper.

Signed Patriots to a 4-year, $68M deal (max $80M). Doubs led the Packers in receiving in 2025 with 55 catches for 724 yards and 6 TDs and was Jordan Love's most trusted target. Departure creates a WR depth hole, though Green Bay has Matthew Golden, Christian Watson, Jayden Reed, and Dontayvion Wicks.

Signed Dolphins to a 3-year, $67.5M deal ($45M guaranteed). Willis went 4-3 in starts/relief appearances and posted a 134.6 passer rating as Love's backup. Departure leaves GB with no proven backup QB.

Still unsigned as of March 13. Walker was Green Bay's starting left tackle for three seasons (48 starts). The Packers prepared for this departure by drafting Jordan Morgan (1st round, 2024) and are ready to transition Morgan to the starting role. Walker reportedly seeking a 1-year deal after failing to find his anticipated multi-year market.

Released by GB (post-June 1 designation), creating $19.5M in cap space. Jenkins suffered a season-ending ankle injury in 2025 and was the starting center/LG. His release was sealed after the Packers re-signed Sean Rhyan and restructured Aaron Banks. Net: GB saves cap but also loses a veteran versatile OL.

Released as post-June 1 cut, saving $8.8M. Hobbs signed a 4-year, $48M deal in 2025 but played just 358 defensive snaps over 11 games due to recurring knee injuries. Was the team's intended CB2 but never healthy enough to fill the role.
Traded to Colts for LB Zaire Franklin. Wooden was the team's starting nose tackle in 2025 after Kenny Clark was traded. His departure was the primary driver for Green Bay's aggressive IDL shopping.

Tender not issued; signed with Miami. Anderson was primarily a special teams ace (top-5 in ST reps two consecutive seasons) and safety depth behind McKinney and Williams. Loss is low-impact on defense given GB's four locked-in safeties, but creates a ST void.
Packers declined his restricted free agent tender. Wilson was RB2/3 and averaged 4.0 YPC in 2025. GB had already re-signed Chris Brooks on a 2-year deal, making Wilson expendable.

signings.analysis: Acquired via trade (sent DT Colby Wooden). Franklin led the NFL in tackles from 2022-2025 and was a 2024 2nd-team All-Pro/Pro Bowler. Two years remain on his deal at ~$7M/yr with no guaranteed money — Green Bay can cut him at $0 dead cap if needed. He was poor in coverage in 2025 (PFF grade 38.4), raising concerns. He is a legitimate starter-caliber player but the contract structure signals a bridge/transitional piece rather than a long-term fix. LB need drops from critical to moderate. Team still needs a long-term answer.
signings.draft_capital_note: GB sent DT Colby Wooden to Colts in the trade

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a 3-year, $33M deal with an $11M signing bonus. Rhyan transitioned from part-time RG to starting center after Elgton Jenkins' ankle injury and excelled over the final 7 regular-season games plus the playoff. The multi-year deal at starter money locks him in as the starting center, replacing Jenkins at a lower cost (~$6M less per year). IOL need at center is fully addressed. The remaining IOL question is Aaron Banks at LG (restructured, under contract), with Jordan Morgan projected to move to LT.

Signed hours after Vikings released him. Two-year, $23M deal with $13M guaranteed in Year 1 and a team option for $10M in Year 2. Hargrave is a 2-time Pro Bowler with 49 career sacks and a direct connection to new DC Jonathan Gannon (posted 18.5 sacks in their two seasons together in Philadelphia). At 33, however, he is not a long-term answer. He addresses the most urgent IDL need as a starter alongside Devonte Wyatt, but his age and declining production (3.5 sacks in 2025) mean the team will still want draft capital at IDL. Crucially, as a cap casualty from the Vikings, his signing does NOT cost the Packers any compensatory pick credit. Need drops from critical to moderate.
signings.draft_capital_note: No compensatory pick impact. Classified as a street free agent.

signings.analysis: Two-year, $10M deal but only $3M guaranteed (signing bonus only). The 2027 year functions as a team option; GB can release him with virtually no dead cap. St-Juste is 6-3 with good length and had a solid 2025 season in LA (68.3 passer rating in coverage). He will compete for the CB2 spot opposite Keisean Nixon, pushing Carrington Valentine. However, only $3M in real guarantees and below-starter-tier AAV makes this a competition/bridge signing, not a franchise cornerstone. CB need drops from critical to moderate — the Packers openly stated they need more additions at the position.
signings.draft_capital_note: Offsets Kingsley Enagbare's 6th-round compensatory pick in the formula.

signings.analysis: One-year deal with base value of $2.5M (max $4.5M with incentives). Moore is primarily a return specialist — he averaged 27.5 yards/kick return and 11.6 yards/punt return in 2025 with the 49ers, nearly double Green Bay's 2025 punt return average of 5.6. He addresses a clear special teams void but is not an answer to the WR starter need left by Doubs' departure. He projects as ST ace/WR5 at best. WR depth need unchanged.
signings.draft_capital_note: One-year deal under $3M threshold — should not offset a compensatory pick.

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a 1-year, $2.5M deal. Cox had 4 sacks and 7 QB hits in 2024 but missed most of 2025 with a groin injury. He provides a 5th rosterable EDGE option but at near-minimum money, his roster spot is not guaranteed. With Parsons recovering from a torn ACL (likely to miss the start of 2026) and Gary/Enagbare gone, Cox will be asked to play a larger role than his contract suggests. EDGE is still a critical need.
signings.draft_capital_note: Re-sign of own RFA; no comp pick impact.

signings.analysis: Re-signed as restricted free agent on a 1-year, $3.75M deal (with $1M in playtime incentives). Kinnard played 285 offensive snaps in 2025 as a fill-in starter at RT when Zach Tom was injured, and also served as a 6th OL/blocking TE. His retention gives GB swing tackle depth behind Jordan Morgan (LT) and Zach Tom (RT, recovering from patella surgery). Not a solution at LT — strictly a depth/versatility signing. OT starter need (LT successor to Rasheed Walker) remains unchanged.
signings.draft_capital_note: Re-sign of own RFA below tender threshold.

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a 2-year, $4.85M deal. Brooks is a blocking-first back who had 27 carries for 106 yards in 2025 but contributed heavily in pass protection and on special teams (team-high 14 ST tackles). With Josh Jacobs still under contract, Brooks re-signing keeps RB depth intact. Not a starter, but plugs the depth gap left by Emanuel Wilson's exit.
signings.draft_capital_note: Re-sign of own RFA at below-minimum-tender figure. No comp pick impact.

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a 1-year minimum deal. Ford returned to GB in December after the Bears waived him and played in the final 3 regular-season games and the playoff loss. His contract does not guarantee him a roster spot. Provides cheap depth at IDL after Colby Wooden was traded away, but is not a starter-caliber option. IDL need remains significant.
signings.draft_capital_note: Re-sign of own RFA. No comp pick impact.

signings.analysis: Re-signed on a 1-year, $2.25M deal (max $3M with incentives). Niemann is a core special teamer with LB depth value. His re-signing is a depth/ST move and does not address the starting LB need.

Signed to a minimum-level contract. Dillard was released by the Titans as a cap casualty after 10 starts in 2025. He previously spent 2024 with Green Bay as a backup and special teamer. This is a depth/camp body signing at LT behind Jordan Morgan. Does not address the starting OT need.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: GB has now lost both Rashan Gary (traded to Cowboys) AND Kingsley Enagbare (Jets). The only edge rushers under contract are: Micah Parsons (recovering from torn ACL, likely to miss the start of 2026), Lukas Van Ness (flashed late in 2025), Brenton Cox Jr. (1-year minimum, missed most of 2025 injured), and Barryn Sorrell/Collin Oliver (developmental). The Packers have essentially one healthy, productive edge rusher for Week 1. This is now a top-tier critical need. They must add EDGE help in the draft — ideally in rounds 1-3.

The need remains elevated. Nate Hobbs (released) and Gary's departure from pass rush compound CB issues. St-Juste (2yr/$10M, only $3M guaranteed) is a competition piece, not a true CB2 solution. Nixon is the only locked-in starter. Carrington Valentine and St-Juste are competing for CB2. The team openly stated they need more additions. CB is still a tier-2 draft priority, likely rounds 1-3.

Hargrave (2yr/$23M) partially addresses the void left by Colby Wooden's trade and Kenny Clark's departure. He will start alongside Devonte Wyatt. However, Hargrave is 33, coming off declining production, and carries only $13M in real guarantees. The 2027 year is a team option. GB will almost certainly draft an IDL in rounds 2-4 for developmental/long-term purposes. Need drops from critical (tier 1) to moderate (tier 2).

Franklin acquisition via trade partially addresses Quay Walker's departure. He is a starter-caliber player who can pair with Edgerrin Cooper, but his 2025 regression was significant and he has zero guaranteed money remaining on his deal. Team can cut him with no dead cap hit. The need is stabilized for 2026 but not resolved long-term. Draft depth at LB in rounds 4-6 remains advisable.

Rasheed Walker (LT) is still unsigned. Jordan Morgan (2024 1st-round pick) is ready to take over at LT, so this is a planned transition, not a crisis. However, Zach Tom (RT) is recovering from patella surgery and is uncertain for Week 1, and Kinnard/Dillard are depth-only options. GB should still draft OT insurance, but this is no longer an urgent need if Morgan develops as expected. Tier-2 need primarily driven by depth and Tom's injury risk.

Rhyan re-signed on a 3-year, $33M deal with $11M guaranteed and will be the starting center. Aaron Banks (LG) was restructured and remains under contract. Jordan Morgan likely moves to LT. The interior OL is now among the most stable units on the roster. IOL is no longer a meaningful draft need.

Romeo Doubs (4yr/$68M, Patriots) was Green Bay's leading receiver in 2025. His departure leaves a gap at WR2. GB does have Christian Watson (extension), Matthew Golden (2025 draft pick), Jayden Reed, and Dontayvion Wicks. Skyy Moore is a ST specialist, not a true WR replacement. The Packers should look at WR in the mid-rounds of the draft to replenish depth and provide competition. WR need escalates from tier 3 to tier 2.

Malik Willis (3yr/$67.5M) departed to Miami. Jordan Love is the unquestioned starter, but GB now has zero proven backup quarterbacks. They will need to address QB depth in the draft or via late-season veteran free agency. Elevated from a non-need to a day-2/day-3 draft consideration.
See above. Hargrave signing partially addresses the need.

key_additions.headline: Two-time Pro Bowler reunites with DC Jonathan Gannon to anchor rebuilt defensive interior

key_additions.contract_summary: Trade (sent Colby Wooden to Colts); 2yr/$14M ($0M gtd remaining)
key_additions.headline: 2024 All-Pro and league tackles leader acquired via trade to replace departing Quay Walker
Emergency-turned-starter at center locked up long-term after excelling to end 2025 season
Physical 6-3 corner signed to compete for CB2 role opposite Keisean Nixon
Elite return specialist (27.5 KR avg, 11.6 PR avg in 2025) fixes Green Bay's worst special teams unit
Injury-plagued pass rusher re-signed for depth in a suddenly barren EDGE room

key_losses.headline: Former 1st-round pick traded away after producing zero sacks in final 9 games of 2025
Green Bay's leading receiver in 2025 cashes in as Patriots' top receiver target
Four-year Packers starter and perennial 100-tackle machine lands with Raiders
Backup QB who posted 134.6 passer rating in Green Bay earns starting job in Miami
Reliable rotational edge rusher departs, leaving Packers with near-zero pass rush depth
Two-time Pro Bowl lineman released after season-ending ankle injury to create $19.5M in cap space
Three-year LT starter departs as Jordan Morgan prepares to take over on blind side
Last year's $48M free-agent cornerback bust released after playing just 358 snaps in injury-plagued season

notes: Rasheed Walker remains unsigned as of March 13, 2026 — the top remaining free agent on the market. His eventual signing destination and contract structure will determine whether GB earns a 2nd or 3rd-round compensatory pick in 2027 (they are projected to earn compensatory picks for Willis, Doubs, Quay Walker, and Walker). The Packers' 2026 draft capital strategy is heavily shaped by comp pick accumulation: they expect 2 third-rounders, a 4th, and a 5th in 2027. Andre Dillard's Packers.com listing appears to be from his 2024 signing; his 2026 re-signing terms are not separately confirmed and are listed at the minimum. All other contract figures are sourced from Spotrac, OverTheCap, ESPN, and beat reporters.
